items.name: Char
items.doc:
A Unicode code point (scalar value from 0 to 0x10FFFF).

Supports character literal syntax: 'a', '\n', '\u{1F600}'.

members.doc:
Returns true if this character has a multi-char uppercase expansion.
For example, German ß uppercases to "SS" (two characters).

members.name: toLowercase
members.doc:
Returns the lowercase version of this character.
Uses full Unicode case mapping tables.

members.name: toTitlecase
members.doc:
Returns the titlecase version of this character.
Titlecase differs from uppercase for some characters (e.g., ligatures).

members.name: toUppercase
members.doc:
Returns the uppercase version of this character.
Uses full Unicode case mapping tables.
For characters with multi-char expansions (e.g., ß → SS), returns the first char.
Use hasUppercaseExpansion() and uppercaseExpansion() for full support.

members.name: uppercaseExpansion
members.doc:
Returns the multi-character uppercase expansion for this character.
Returns empty string if no expansion exists.

members.doc: Returns the number of bytes needed to encode this char in UTF-8 (1-4).

items.name: FormatOptions
items.doc:
Options for controlling formatted output.
Used by Formattable and string interpolation syntax: "\{expr:spec}"

Format spec mini-language:
  [[fill]align][sign][#][0][width][.precision][type]

Where type is one of:
  Integers: 'b' (binary), 'o' (octal), 'x' (hex lower), 'X' (hex upper)
  Floats:   'f' (fixed), 'e' (scientific), 'E' (scientific upper), '%' (percent)
  Any:      '?' (debug)

Examples:
  "\{n:>8}"      right-align, width 8
  "\{n:08x}"     zero-pad, width 8, hex
  "\{n:#X}"      hex upper with 0x prefix
  "\{pi:.2}"     precision 2 decimal places
  "\{pi:.2e}"    scientific with 2 decimal places
  "\{ratio:%}"   as percentage (0.5 -> "50%")
  "\{name:^10}"  center, width 10
  "\{value:?}"   debug format

members.name: alignment
members.doc: Text alignment within the field width.

members.name: alternate
members.doc: Alternate form: show 0x/0b/0o prefix for non-decimal radix.

members.name: debug
members.doc: Debug mode: show structural representation.

members.name: default
members.doc: Default format options.

members.name: fill
members.doc: Character used for padding (default: ' ').

members.name: floatStyle
members.doc: Float display style (fixed, scientific, percent).

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates default format options.

members.name: precision
members.doc: For floats: decimal places. For strings: max characters.

members.name: radix
members.doc: Numeric base: 2 (binary), 8 (octal), 10 (decimal), 16 (hex).

members.name: sign
members.doc: How to display the sign for numbers.

members.name: uppercase
members.doc: Use uppercase for hex digits (A-F vs a-f).

members.name: width
members.doc: Minimum field width.

members.name: equals
members.doc: Compares two format options for equality.

items.name: Formattable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be formatted as a string.
Used by print functions and string interpolation.

String interpolation syntax:
  "\{expr}"        uses default formatting
  "\{expr:spec}"   uses format spec (parsed into FormatOptions)

Examples:
  "\{name}"        "Alice"
  "\{name:>10}"    "     Alice"
  "\{n:08x}"       "0000002a"
  "\{pi:.2}"       "3.14"
  "\{value:?}"     debug representation

members.name: format
members.doc: Returns this value formatted as a string with the given options.

items.name: String
items.doc:
A UTF-8 encoded, dynamically sized string with copy-on-write semantics.

Strings are immutable by default; mutations create a new copy if shared.

members.name: format
members.doc:
Returns the string formatted with the given options.

Supports width and alignment formatting.

Example:
    "test".format(options: .{width: .Some(10), alignment: .Left})   // "test      "
    "test".format(options: .{width: .Some(10), alignment: .Right})  // "      test"
    "test".format(options: .{width: .Some(10), alignment: .Center}) // "   test   "
